steps.title: Separate the mission sentence
steps.content: The first idea says the shuttle commenced Mission STS-72. The next idea begins with the introductory participial phrase Lasting eight days and twenty-two hours and then states when the mission ended. Those ideas need a clear sentence boundary before the introductory phrase.

steps.title: Avoid comma splices
steps.content: Choice B leaves the two ideas joined only by a comma, and Choice C gives no punctuation boundary. Choice D creates a comma splice between it lasted and the mission ended. A period after the mission name lets the second sentence begin correctly with the introductory phrase. The answer is A.
